bibliographic_data.title: Neural Machine Translation for Extremely Low-Resource African Languages: A Case Study on Bambara
bibliographic_data.abstract: Low-resource languages present unique challenges to (neural) machine translation. We discuss the case of Bambara, a Mande language for which training data is scarce and requires significant amounts of pre-processing. More than the linguistic situation of Bambara itself, the socio-cultural context within which Bambara speakers live poses challenges for automated processing of this language. In this paper, we present the first parallel data set for machine translation of Bambara into and from English and French and the first benchmark results on machine translation to and from Bambara. We discuss challenges in working with low-resource languages and propose strategies to cope with data scarcity in low-resource machine translation (MT).

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper presents the first parallel data set for machine translation of Bambara into and from English and French, along with benchmark results.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors collected and processed a large volume of text in Bambara to create a parallel corpus suitable for neural machine translation (NMT). They also proposed strategies to cope with data scarcity in low-resource languages.
outcomes: First parallel data set for Bambara
Benchmark results on machine translation to/from Bambara
core_content.full_text_markdown: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) has advanced rapidly over the last ten years, but it remains challenging to apply this technology to underresourced languages. This paper addresses the case of Bambara, a Mande language spoken by millions in Mali and other parts of Africa. The authors present the first parallel data set for machine translation of Bambara into and from English and French, along with benchmark results. They discuss challenges in working with low-resource languages and propose strategies to cope with data scarcity in NMT. The socio-cultural context of Bambara is also discussed, highlighting its implications for model and data development.
